goal: Mount an Ouster OS1-32 on AMR base_link at h=0.42 m with datasheet-matching beams/FOV/rate.
thoughts: Never generic-LIDAR this — cite the OS1-32 datasheet: 32 channels, V-FOV -16.6° to +16.6° (33.2° total), 10 or 20 Hz, ~655k pts/s at 20 Hz (200k at 10). Attach on a child xform 'lidar_mount' under base_link, not at world origin. TF chain: base_link → lidar_mount → laser (optical). Leave 2° tilt as set_attribute after mount if asked.
code:
# 1. Datasheet values — no guessing
spec = lookup_product_spec(product="Ouster OS1-32")
# Expect: channels=32, v_fov=(-16.6, 16.6), rate_hz=[10,20], points_per_sec=~655000

# 2. Mount xform under base_link
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/amr/base_link/lidar_mount",
    prim_type="Xform",
    position=[0.0, 0.0, 0.42],                    # datasheet h=0.42 m
    rotation_euler=[0.0, 0.0, 0.0]                # tilt added separately if needed
)

# 3. Attach RTX LIDAR with OS1-32 preset / explicit datasheet params
add_sensor_to_prim(
    parent_prim_path="/World/amr/base_link/lidar_mount",
    sensor_type="rtx_lidar",
    config_preset="Ouster_OS1_32",                # prefer preset if available
    channels=32,
    v_fov_deg=[-16.6, 16.6],
    rate_hz=10,
    frame_id="laser"
)

inspect_camera(prim_path="/World/amr/base_link/lidar_mount/lidar")

# TF chain to publish later: base_link → lidar_mount → laser
failure_modes: Using generic RTX LIDAR defaults instead of OS1-32 datasheet
Mounting at world origin — AMR moves, sensor stays
Forgetting to name the TF frame_id — Nav2 drops the scan
Swapping to OS1-64 without rebuilding OmniGraph publisher
